proposals.title: [auto-proposed] Fix root cause of "docker-compose startup timeout" in fixture-owner/fixture-repo
proposals.body:
## Summary

Commander detected the same stuck pattern on `fixture-owner/fixture-repo` 3 times across 3 distinct tickets. Workers keep hitting the same wall; fix the root cause so the next worker doesn't.

## Evidence

- Pattern: `docker-compose startup timeout`
- Occurrences: 3 (threshold: 3)
- Distinct tickets: 3 (threshold: 2)
- Source logs: see `evidence_refs` in the proposal JSON

## Suggested tier

T2 — new change addressing a repo-specific repeat failure. Commander review gate applies.

## Notes for the worker head

- Read the evidence logs before planning. The pattern string is the literal signal; the fix should address whatever the workers actually hit.
- If the fix is a new skill or doc rather than code, that's fine — the bar is "next worker doesn't hit this," not "code must change."
- If in doubt, escalate.

proposals.title: [auto-proposed] Promote memory entry "learnings-CLI.md#npm install strips peer markers" to repo skill
proposals.body:
## Summary

Memory entry `learnings-CLI.md#npm install strips peer markers` has been cited 6 times (threshold: 5). It is a promotion candidate — move it from commander memory to the target repo's `.claude/skills/` so every agent inherits it, not just commander workers.

## Evidence

- Key: `learnings-CLI.md#npm install strips peer markers`
- Cited count: 6
- Last cited: 2026-04-16
- Tickets: see `evidence_refs` in the proposal JSON
- Source file: `learnings-CLI.md`

## Suggested tier

T1 — documentation / skill promotion. Clean flow once the target repo is known.

## Notes for the worker head

See `memory/memory-lifecycle.md` "Promotion flow (memory → repo skill)" for the mechanics. The skill file goes in the target repo's `.claude/skills/`; once that PR merges, commander removes the entry from `learnings-<repo>.md` with a trailing back-link.
